1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Long ago God spoke to our ancestors many times and in many ways through the prophets.
But in these last days he has spoken to us through his Son. God made his Son the heir of everything, and through the Son he made the whole universe.

The Son is the shining brightness of God's glory and the exact likeness of who God is. The Son holds everything together by his powerful word. After he had made us clean from our sins, he sat down at the right hand of God, the Majesty in heaven.
So the Son became as much greater than the angels as the name he was given is greater than theirs.
For to which of the angels did God ever say: You are my Son; today I have become your Father? Or: I will be his Father, and he will be my Son?
And again, when God brings his firstborn Son into the world, he says: Let all God's angels worship him.
About the angels he says: He makes his angels winds, and his servants flames of fire.
But about the Son he says: Your throne, O God, will last forever and ever. You will rule your kingdom with what is right.
You have loved what is right and hated what is wrong. So God, your God, has chosen you above your companions and filled you with the oil of joy.
He also says: In the beginning, Lord, you made the earth, and the heavens are the work of your hands.
They will all disappear, but you will remain. They will all wear out like clothes.
You will roll them up like a coat, and they will be changed like clothes. But you stay the same, and your years will never end.
And to which of the angels did God ever say: Sit at my right hand until I put your enemies under your feet?
Are not all the angels spirits who serve God, sent to help those who will receive salvation?

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So we must pay very careful attention to what we have heard, so that we do not drift away from it.
The message God spoke through angels held firm, and every sin and act of disobedience received the punishment it deserved.
So how will we escape if we ignore such a great salvation? The Lord himself first announced this salvation, and those who heard him proved it to us.
God also showed it was true by signs, wonders, and all kinds of miracles, and by the gifts of the Holy Spirit, given as he chose.
God did not put the world to come — the world we are talking about — under the control of angels.
Instead, somewhere the Scripture says: What is man, that you think of him? What is the son of man, that you care for him?
You made him a little lower than the angels. You crowned him with glory and honor,
and put everything under his control. When God put everything under him, he left nothing that is not under him. But right now we do not yet see everything under his control.

What we do see is Jesus, who was made a little lower than the angels for a while. Now he is crowned with glory and honor because he suffered death, so that by God's grace he might taste death for everyone.
God, for whom and through whom everything exists, wanted to bring many children into glory. So it was right for God to make the leader of their salvation perfect through suffering.
Both Jesus, who makes people holy, and those who are made holy belong to the same family. That is why Jesus is not ashamed to call them brothers and sisters.
He says: I will tell my brothers and sisters about you; in the meeting of your people I will sing your praises.
And again: I will put my trust in him. And again: Here am I, and the children God has given me.
Since these children are people of flesh and blood, Jesus also became flesh and blood. He did this so that by his death he could destroy the one who holds the power of death — that is, the devil —
and set free those who were held as slaves all their lives by their fear of death.
For it is clear that Jesus does not help angels, but he helps the descendants of Abraham.
So Jesus had to become like his brothers and sisters in every way, so that he could be a merciful and faithful high priest before God, and so that he could pay for the sins of the people.
Because Jesus himself suffered when he was tempted, he is able to help those who are being tempted.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So then, holy brothers and sisters, who share in the heavenly calling, think carefully about Jesus, the Apostle and High Priest whom we say we believe in.
Jesus was faithful to the God who appointed him, just as Moses was faithful in all God's house.
But Jesus deserves more glory than Moses, just as the builder of a house deserves more honor than the house itself.
Every house is built by someone, but God is the builder of everything.
Moses was faithful as a servant in all God's house, pointing to the things God would say later.
But Christ is faithful as a Son over God's house. And we are his house, if we hold on tightly to our courage and the hope we are proud of.
So, as the Holy Spirit says: Today, if you hear his voice,
do not harden your hearts as you did when you turned against God, on the day you tested him in the desert.
There your ancestors tested me and tried me, even though for forty years they saw what I did.
That is why I was angry with that generation. I said: Their hearts are always going the wrong way, and they have not known my ways.
So in my anger I made a promise: They will never enter my rest.
So be careful, brothers and sisters, that none of you has a sinful, unbelieving heart that turns away from the living God.
Instead, encourage one another every day, as long as it is called Today, so that none of you will be hardened by the lies of sin.
We share in Christ if we hold firmly to the end the confidence we had at the start.
As the Scripture says: Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts as you did when you turned against God.
Who were those who heard and turned against God? Were they not all the people Moses led out of Egypt?
And with whom was God angry for forty years? Was it not with those who sinned, whose bodies fell dead in the desert?
And to whom did God promise that they would never enter his rest? Was it not to those who did not obey?
So we see that they could not enter, because they did not believe.

4.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So while the promise of entering God's rest still stands, let us be careful that none of you fails to reach it.
For the good news was preached to us, just as it was to them. But the message they heard did them no good, because they did not join it with faith.
We who have believed do enter that rest. As God said: So in my anger I made a promise: They will never enter my rest. And yet God's work was finished from the time he made the world.
Somewhere the Scripture speaks about the seventh day like this: And on the seventh day God rested from all his work.
And again, in the verse above, God says: They will never enter my rest.
So it is still true that some will enter that rest. But those who first heard the good news did not enter, because they did not obey.
So God again set a certain day, calling it Today. Long afterward, he spoke through David the words already quoted: Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts.
For if Joshua had given them rest, God would not have spoken later about another day.
So there is still a Sabbath rest waiting for the people of God.
For anyone who enters God's rest also rests from his own work, just as God rested from his.
So let us do our best to enter that rest, so that no one will fall by following the same example of disobedience.

For the word of God is alive and active. It is sharper than any sword with two edges. It cuts deep enough to divide soul and spirit, joints and marrow, and it judges the thoughts and attitudes of the heart.
Nothing in all creation is hidden from God. Everything is uncovered and open before the eyes of the One we must answer to.
So then, since we have a great high priest who has gone into heaven — Jesus the Son of God — let us hold firmly to the faith we say we believe.
For our high priest is able to understand our weaknesses. He was tempted in every way that we are, yet he never sinned.
So let us come with confidence to God's throne of grace, so that we may receive mercy and find grace to help us when we need it.

5.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Every high priest is chosen from among the people and is appointed to act for them before God. He offers gifts and sacrifices for sins.
He is able to be gentle with those who are ignorant and who go the wrong way, because he himself is weak too.
That is why he has to offer sacrifices for his own sins as well as for the sins of the people.
And no one takes this honor for himself. He must be called by God, just as Aaron was.
In the same way, Christ did not take on himself the glory of becoming a high priest. God gave it to him, saying: You are my Son; today I have become your Father.
And in another place God says: You are a priest forever, in the line of Melchizedek.
While Jesus lived on earth, he prayed and pleaded, with loud cries and tears, to the One who could save him from death. And God heard him because of his deep respect for God.
Even though he was the Son, Jesus learned obedience from what he suffered.
And once he was made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation for all who obey him.
And God named him a high priest in the line of Melchizedek.
We have a lot to say about this, but it is hard to explain, because you have become slow to understand.
By now you ought to be teachers, but instead you need someone to teach you again the basic truths of God's word. You need milk, not solid food!
Anyone who lives on milk is still a baby and does not know much about what is right.
But solid food is for grown-ups, who have trained themselves to tell the difference between good and evil.

6.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So let us move on from the basic teachings about Christ and go on to maturity. Let us not lay the foundation all over again — turning away from deeds that lead to death, having faith in God,
teaching about baptisms and laying on of hands, the rising of the dead, and eternal judgment.
And, God willing, this is what we will do.
It is impossible to bring back to a change of heart those who once understood the truth, who tasted the heavenly gift, who shared in the Holy Spirit,
who tasted the goodness of God's word and the powers of the coming world,
and who then fall away. They cannot be brought back, because they are nailing the Son of God to the cross all over again and shaming him in public.
Land that drinks in the rain often falling on it, and that produces a good crop for those who farm it, receives God's blessing.
But land that produces thorns and weeds is worthless and in danger of being cursed. In the end it will be burned.
Dear friends, even though we talk like this, we are sure of better things in your case — things that come with salvation.
God is not unfair. He will not forget your work and the love you have shown him as you helped his people, and as you keep on helping them.
We want each of you to keep showing this same effort to the very end, so that you will be sure of your hope.
We do not want you to become lazy. Instead, be like those who, through faith and patience, receive what God promised.
When God made his promise to Abraham, he swore by himself, because there was no one greater to swear by.
He said: I will surely bless you and give you many descendants.
And so, after waiting patiently, Abraham received what was promised.
People swear by someone greater than themselves, and the oath settles every argument.
So when God wanted to make it very clear to the heirs of his promise that his purpose would never change, he guaranteed it with an oath.
God did this so that, by two unchangeable things in which it is impossible for God to lie, we who have run to him for safety would be greatly encouraged to hold on to the hope set before us.
We have this hope as an anchor for the soul, firm and secure. It reaches behind the curtain into God's presence,
where Jesus has gone ahead of us. He has become a high priest forever, in the line of Melchizedek.

7.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: This Melchizedek was king of Salem and a priest of God Most High. He met Abraham as Abraham was returning from defeating the kings, and he blessed Abraham.
And Abraham gave him a tenth of everything. First, the name Melchizedek means “king of what is right.” Also, “king of Salem” means “king of peace.”
We have no record of his father, his mother, or his family, and no record of his birth or his death. Like the Son of God, he remains a priest forever.
Think how great Melchizedek was! Even Abraham, the great ancestor, gave him a tenth of what he had taken in battle.
Now the law says that the priests, who are descendants of Levi, must collect a tenth from the people — that is, from their own relatives, even though they too are descendants of Abraham.
But Melchizedek was not a descendant of Levi, yet he collected a tenth from Abraham and blessed the man who had God's promises.
And there is no question that the greater person blesses the lesser.
In the one case, the tenth is collected by priests who die. But in the other case, it is collected by Melchizedek, who is said to be still living.
You could even say that Levi, who collects the tenth, paid the tenth through Abraham,
because Levi was still in the body of his ancestor Abraham when Melchizedek met him.

Now if the priesthood of Levi could have made people perfect (and the people were given the law based on it), why was there still a need for another priest to come — one in the line of Melchizedek, not in the line of Aaron?
For when the priesthood is changed, the law must be changed too.
The one we are talking about belonged to a different tribe, and no one from that tribe has ever served at the altar.
It is clear that our Lord came from the tribe of Judah, and Moses said nothing about priests coming from that tribe.
And this becomes even clearer: another priest like Melchizedek has appeared,
one who became a priest not because of a rule about human ancestry, but because of the power of a life that cannot be destroyed.
For the Scripture says about him: You are a priest forever, in the line of Melchizedek.
So the old rule is set aside, because it was weak and useless.
(The law never made anything perfect.) And a better hope is brought in, by which we come near to God.
And it did not happen without an oath! Others became priests without any oath,
but Jesus became a priest with an oath, when God said to him: The Lord has sworn and will not change his mind: You are a priest forever.
Because of this oath, Jesus is the guarantee of a better agreement.
Now there have been many of those other priests, because death kept them from staying in office.
But Jesus lives forever, so his work as priest never passes to anyone else.
So he is able to save completely those who come to God through him, because he always lives to speak to God for them.
This is the kind of high priest we needed: holy, blameless, pure, set apart from sinners, and lifted higher than the heavens.

Unlike the other high priests, he does not need to offer sacrifices day after day, first for his own sins and then for the sins of the people. He sacrificed for their sins once for all when he offered himself.
The law appoints as high priests men who are weak. But the oath, which came after the law, appointed the Son, who has been made perfect forever.

8.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Here is the main point of what we are saying: We have a high priest like this, and he sat down at the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in heaven.
He serves in the holy place, in the true tent of worship that the Lord set up, not any human being.
Every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices, so this high priest also had to have something to offer.
If he were on earth, he would not be a priest at all, since there are already priests who offer the gifts the law requires.

They serve at a place that is only a copy and shadow of what is in heaven. That is why God warned Moses, when he was about to build the tent: Be sure to make everything just like the pattern I showed you on the mountain.
But the work Jesus has been given is far greater, just as the agreement he brings is better, because it is built on better promises.
For if there had been nothing wrong with that first agreement, there would have been no need for a second one.
But God found fault with the people and said: The days are coming, says the Lord, when I will make a new agreement with the people of Israel and the people of Judah.

It will not be like the agreement I made with their ancestors when I took them by the hand to lead them out of Egypt. They did not stay faithful to my agreement, so I turned away from them, says the Lord.

This is the agreement I will make with the people of Israel after that time, says the Lord: I will put my laws in their minds and write them on their hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
No longer will anyone need to teach his neighbor or his brother, saying: Know the Lord. For they will all know me, from the least of them to the greatest.
I will forgive their wrong deeds and remember their sins no more.
By calling this agreement new, God has made the first one old. And what is old and worn out will soon disappear.

9.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Now the first agreement had rules for worship and a holy place here on earth.
A tent was set up. In its first room were the lampstand, the table, and the holy bread. This room was called the Holy Place.
Behind the second curtain was the room called the Most Holy Place.
It had the golden altar for incense and the gold-covered ark of the agreement. Inside the ark were the gold jar of manna, Aaron's staff that had budded, and the stone tablets of the agreement.
Above the ark were the glorious winged creatures, whose wings spread over the place where sins were forgiven. But we cannot talk about all of this in detail now.
When everything was set up like this, the priests went regularly into the first room to do their work.
But only the high priest went into the second room, and only once a year, and never without blood, which he offered for himself and for the sins the people had done without knowing.
The Holy Spirit was showing by this that the way into the Most Holy Place had not yet been opened while the first tent was still standing.
This is a picture for the present time. It shows that the gifts and sacrifices being offered could not clear the conscience of the worshiper.
They were only about food and drink and various washings — outward rules that applied until the time when God would set things right.
But Christ came as the high priest of the good things that are now here. He went through the greater and more perfect tent, one not made by human hands, that is, not part of this created world.
He did not enter by the blood of goats and calves. He entered the Most Holy Place once for all by his own blood, and so he set us free forever.
The blood of goats and bulls, and the ashes of a young cow sprinkled on those who are unclean, make them clean on the outside.

So how much more will the blood of Christ clean our consciences from deeds that lead to death, so that we may serve the living God! Through the eternal Spirit, Christ offered himself to God, with no fault at all.

This is why Christ is the go-between of a new agreement. He died to set people free from the sins they committed under the first agreement, so that those whom God has called may receive the eternal inheritance he promised.
Now where there is a will, there has to be proof that the person who made it has died.
A will takes effect only after the person dies; it is never in force while that person is still alive.
That is why even the first agreement was not put into effect without blood.

When Moses had announced every command of the law to all the people, he took the blood of calves and goats, together with water, red wool, and a hyssop branch, and sprinkled the scroll and all the people.
He said: This is the blood of the agreement that God has commanded you to keep.
In the same way, he sprinkled with the blood both the tent and everything used in worship.
In fact, the law requires that almost everything be cleansed with blood, and without the shedding of blood there is no forgiveness.
So the copies of the heavenly things had to be made clean with these sacrifices. But the heavenly things themselves needed better sacrifices than these.
For Christ did not enter a holy place made by human hands, which is only a copy of the true one. He entered heaven itself, to appear now before God for us.
Nor did he enter heaven to offer himself again and again, the way the high priest enters the Most Holy Place every year with blood that is not his own.
If that were so, Christ would have had to suffer many times since the world was made. But he has appeared once for all, at the end of the ages, to take away sin by sacrificing himself.
People are appointed to die once, and after that to face judgment.
In the same way, Christ was offered once to take away the sins of many. And he will appear a second time, not to deal with sin, but to bring salvation to those who are waiting for him.

10.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: The law is only a shadow of the good things that are coming, not the real things themselves. So it can never make perfect those who come to worship, even with the same sacrifices offered year after year.
If it could, would they not have stopped offering them? For the worshipers would have been cleansed once for all, and would no longer feel guilty for their sins.
Instead, those sacrifices are a yearly reminder of sins,
because it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
So when Christ came into the world, he said: You did not want sacrifices and offerings, but you prepared a body for me.
You were not pleased with burnt offerings and sin offerings.
Then I said: Here I am — it is written about me in the scroll — I have come to do your will, O God.
First he said: You did not want sacrifices and offerings, burnt offerings and sin offerings, and you were not pleased with them — even though the law required them.
Then he said: Here I am; I have come to do your will. So God sets aside the first to put the second in its place.
And because of God's will, we have been made holy through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
Day after day every priest stands and does his work, offering again and again the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins.
But Jesus offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, and then he sat down at the right hand of God.
Since that time he waits for his enemies to be put under his feet.
For by one sacrifice he has made perfect forever those who are being made holy.
The Holy Spirit also tells us this. First he says:
This is the agreement I will make with them after that time, says the Lord: I will put my laws in their hearts and write them in their minds.
Then he adds: I will remember their sins and wrong deeds no more.
And where these have been forgiven, there is no longer any need to offer sacrifice for sin.
So, brothers and sisters, we can now enter the Most Holy Place with confidence, because of the blood of Jesus.
He opened a new and living way for us through the curtain — that is, through his body.
And since we have a great priest over God's house,
let us come near to God with a sincere heart and a sure faith, our hearts sprinkled clean from a guilty conscience and our bodies washed with pure water.
Let us hold tightly to the hope we say we have, without wavering, because the One who promised is faithful.
And let us think about how we can stir up one another to love and good works.
Let us not give up meeting together, as some are in the habit of doing. Instead, let us encourage one another, all the more as you see the day coming.
If we keep on sinning on purpose after we have come to know the truth, then there is no longer any sacrifice that can cover those sins.
There is only a terrible fear of judgment and of a raging fire that will destroy God's enemies.
Anyone who broke the law of Moses died without mercy on the word of two or three witnesses.

How much worse, do you think, will the punishment be for those who have trampled the Son of God underfoot, who have treated the blood of the agreement as something unholy — the very blood that made them holy — and who have insulted the Spirit of grace?
For we know the One who said: It is mine to pay back; I will repay. And again: The Lord will judge his people.
It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
Remember those earlier days, after you first understood the truth, when you stood your ground through a hard struggle and much suffering.
Sometimes you were openly shamed by insults and trouble; at other times you stood side by side with those who were treated that way.
You suffered along with those in prison, and you joyfully accepted the taking of your property, because you knew that you had better and lasting riches.
So do not throw away your confidence; it will be greatly rewarded.
You need to keep going, so that after you have done the will of God, you will receive what he has promised.
For, in just a little while: The one who is coming will come and will not delay.
And: The person who is right with me will live by faith. But if he turns back, I will not be pleased with him.
But we are not those who turn back and are lost. We are those who believe and are saved.

11.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Faith is being sure of what we hope for, and certain of what we cannot see.
This is what the people of long ago were praised for.
By faith we understand that the universe was made by God's word, so that what we see was made out of what cannot be seen.
By faith Abel offered God a better sacrifice than Cain did. By his faith Abel was accepted as a good man, because God accepted his gifts. And even though Abel is dead, by his faith he still speaks.
By faith Enoch was taken up so that he did not die. He could not be found, because God had taken him. Before he was taken, he was praised as one who pleased God.
And without faith it is impossible to please God. Anyone who comes to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards those who sincerely look for him.

By faith Noah, when God warned him about things not yet seen, took God seriously and built an ark to save his family. By his faith he showed the world to be wrong, and he received the right standing that comes by faith.
By faith Abraham obeyed when God called him to go to a place he would later receive as his own. He set out, even though he did not know where he was going.
By faith he lived as a stranger in the land God had promised him. He lived in tents, and so did Isaac and Jacob, who shared the same promise.
For Abraham was looking forward to the city with foundations, whose builder and maker is God.
By faith Abraham — and Sarah herself, who was too old — were given the power to have a child, because they trusted the One who had made the promise.
And so from this one man, who was as good as dead, came descendants as many as the stars in the sky and as countless as the sand on the seashore.

All these people were still living by faith when they died. They did not receive the things that were promised; they only saw them and welcomed them from far away. They agreed that they were strangers and travelers on the earth.
People who say such things show that they are looking for a country of their own.
If they had been thinking of the country they had left, they could have gone back.
Instead, they were longing for a better country — a heavenly one. So God is not ashamed to be called their God, for he has prepared a city for them.
By faith Abraham, when God tested him, offered Isaac as a sacrifice. He who had received the promises was ready to offer his only son,
even though God had told him: Your descendants will come through Isaac.
Abraham reasoned that God could even raise the dead. And in a way, he did receive Isaac back from death.
By faith Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau as he spoke about their future.
By faith Jacob, when he was dying, blessed each of Joseph's sons. He worshiped God as he leaned on the top of his staff.
By faith Joseph, near the end of his life, spoke about the time when the people of Israel would leave Egypt, and he gave instructions about his bones.
By faith Moses' parents hid him for three months after he was born, because they saw he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid of the king's command.
By faith Moses, when he had grown up, refused to be known as the son of Pharaoh's daughter.
He chose to suffer along with the people of God rather than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a short time.
He thought it was worth more to be insulted for Christ than to have all the treasures of Egypt, because he was looking ahead to his reward.
By faith he left Egypt, not afraid of the king's anger. He kept going, as if he could see the God who cannot be seen.
By faith he kept the Passover and sprinkled the blood, so that the destroying angel would not touch the firstborn of Israel.
By faith the people passed through the Red Sea as if on dry land. But when the Egyptians tried to do the same, they were drowned.
By faith the walls of Jericho fell down, after the people had marched around them for seven days.
By faith Rahab the prostitute was not killed with those who refused to obey, because she had welcomed the spies in peace.
What more should I say? I do not have time to tell about Gideon, Barak, Samson, and Jephthah, or about David, Samuel, and the prophets.
Through faith they conquered kingdoms, did what was right, and received what God promised. They shut the mouths of lions,
put out raging fires, and escaped being killed by the sword. They were weak, but they were made strong. They became powerful in battle and drove back enemy armies.
Women received back their loved ones, raised from death. Others were tortured and refused to be set free, so that they could rise to a better life.
Some were laughed at and beaten; some were even chained and put in prison.
They were stoned, they were sawn in two, they were killed with the sword. They went around in sheepskins and goatskins, poor, mistreated, and badly treated.
The world was not good enough for them. They wandered in deserts and mountains, and lived in caves and holes in the ground.
All these people were praised for their faith, yet none of them received what God had promised.
God had planned something better for us, so that they would not be made perfect without us.

12.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So then, since we are surrounded by such a great crowd of witnesses, let us throw off everything that holds us back, and the sin that so easily traps us. Let us keep running, without giving up, the race that is set before us.
Let us keep our eyes on Jesus, who began our faith and makes it complete. For the joy ahead of him, he accepted the cross and ignored its shame, and now he sits at the right hand of God's throne.
Think about Jesus, who put up with such hatred from sinful people, so that you will not grow tired and lose heart.
In your fight against sin, you have not yet had to give your blood.
And you have forgotten the encouraging words God speaks to you as his children: My child, do not take lightly the Lord's correction, and do not lose heart when he corrects you.
For the Lord corrects the one he loves, and he disciplines everyone he accepts as his child.
Put up with hard times as God's correction. God is treating you as his children. For what child is never corrected by his father?
If you are not corrected (and everyone is corrected), then you are not really his children at all.
Besides, our human fathers corrected us, and we respected them for it. How much more should we obey our spiritual Father and live!
Our fathers corrected us for a little while, as they thought best. But God corrects us for our good, so that we may share in his holiness.
No correction seems pleasant at the time, but painful. Later, though, it produces a harvest of peace and right living for those who have been trained by it.
So strengthen your tired arms and your weak knees.
Make straight paths for your feet, so that the lame foot will not be put out of joint, but rather healed.
Do everything you can to live in peace with everyone, and to be holy. Without holiness no one will see the Lord.
Be careful that no one misses out on God's grace, and that no bitter root grows up to cause trouble and to harm many.
Make sure no one is sexually immoral or godless like Esau, who sold his rights as the oldest son for a single meal.
As you know, later, when he wanted to receive the blessing, he was turned away. He could not undo what he had done, even though he begged for the blessing with tears.
You have not come to a mountain that can be touched, that burns with fire, with darkness, gloom, and storm.
You have not come to a blast of a trumpet, or to a voice speaking words that made the people beg not to hear another word.
They could not bear the command: If even an animal touches the mountain, it must be stoned.
The sight was so terrifying that Moses said: I am trembling with fear.
Instead, you have come to Mount Zion, to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem. You have come to thousands and thousands of angels, gathered with joy.
You have come to the meeting of God's firstborn children, whose names are written in heaven. You have come to God, the Judge of all, and to the spirits of good people who have been made perfect.
You have come to Jesus, the go-between of a new agreement, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks of better things than the blood of Abel.

See that you do not refuse the One who is speaking. If the people did not escape when they refused the One who warned them on earth, how much less will we escape if we turn away from the One who warns us from heaven?
At that time his voice shook the earth, but now he has promised: Once more I will shake not only the earth, but also the heavens.
The words “once more” show that what can be shaken — that is, created things — will be removed, so that what cannot be shaken will remain.
So then, since we are receiving a kingdom that cannot be shaken, let us be thankful. And let us worship God in a way that pleases him, with deep respect and wonder,
for our God is like a fire that burns everything up.

13.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Keep on loving each other as brothers and sisters.
Do not forget to welcome strangers, for by doing this some people have welcomed angels without knowing it.
Remember those in prison, as if you were in prison with them. Remember those who are mistreated, since you also have bodies that can suffer.
Marriage should be honored by everyone, and husbands and wives should be faithful to each other. God will judge those who are sexually immoral and those who are unfaithful in marriage.
Keep your lives free from the love of money, and be happy with what you have, because God has said: I will never leave you; I will never give up on you.
So we can say with confidence: The Lord is my helper; I will not be afraid. What can anyone do to me?
Remember your leaders, who spoke God's word to you. Think about how their lives turned out, and copy their faith.
Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
Do not be pulled away by all kinds of strange teachings. It is good for the heart to be made strong by grace, not by rules about food, which have never helped those who follow them.
We have an altar, and the priests who serve at the tent have no right to eat from it.
The high priest brings the blood of animals into the Most Holy Place as an offering for sin, but the bodies of those animals are burned outside the camp.
In the same way, Jesus also suffered outside the city gate, to make the people holy through his own blood.
So let us go out to him outside the camp and carry the shame he carried.
For here we have no city that lasts, but we are looking for the city that is coming.
So through Jesus let us always offer to God a sacrifice of praise — the words of our lips that thank his name.
And do not forget to do good and to share with others, for these are the kinds of sacrifices that please God.

Obey your leaders and follow their lead, because they watch over you as people who must give an account to God. Obey them so that their work will be a joy, not a heavy burden, for that would be no help to you.
Pray for us. We are sure that we have a clear conscience and want to live honestly in every way.
And I beg you especially to pray that I will be brought back to you soon.
Now may the God of peace, who brought back from death our Lord Jesus, the great Shepherd of the sheep, through the blood of the eternal agreement,
give you everything good you need to do his will. And may God do in us what pleases him, through Jesus Christ. To him be glory forever and ever. Amen.
Brothers and sisters, I beg you to put up with this message of encouragement, for I have written to you only a short letter.
I want you to know that our brother Timothy has been set free. If he comes soon, I will bring him with me when I see you.
Greet all your leaders and all God's people. The believers from Italy send you their greetings.
May grace be with you all. Amen.
